---
title: "flat_maps.Rmd"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r development, include=FALSE}
library(testthat)
```

# fix_bbox_aspect
 
```{r function-fix_bbox_aspect}
#' @noRd
fix_bbox_aspect <- function(xlim, ylim, target_ratio = 1) {
  # target_ratio = largeur / hauteur du panneau final
  
  mid_lat <- mean(ylim)
  lon_factor <- cos(mid_lat * pi / 180)
  
  width  <- diff(xlim) * lon_factor
  height <- diff(ylim)
  
  current_ratio <- width / height
  
  if (current_ratio > target_ratio) {
    # bbox trop large -> augmenter la hauteur
    new_height <- width / target_ratio
    delta <- (new_height - height) / 2
    ylim <- ylim + c(-delta, delta)
  } else {
    # bbox trop haute -> augmenter la largeur
    new_width <- height * target_ratio
    delta <- (new_width - width) / lon_factor / 2
    xlim <- xlim + c(-delta, delta)
  }
  
  list(xlim = xlim, ylim = ylim)
}
```

# fct_build_and_save_basemap

```{r function-fct_build_and_save_basemap}
#' Create and save OpenStreetMap base map for an estuary
#'
#' @param data Data frame containing latitude and longitude
#' @param estuary_name Character name of estuary (used for folder name)
#' @param villes_selection Character vector of city names to keep (optional)
#' @param path Base path for saving (default: inst/extdata/basemaps)
#'
#' @return A list with map components (also saved on disk)
#'
#' @import dplyr
#' @importFrom sf st_as_sf st_bbox st_polygon st_sf st_sfc
#' @importFrom sf st_intersection st_distance st_write
#' @importFrom osmdata opq add_osm_feature osmdata_sf
#' @importFrom maptiles get_tiles
#' @importFrom terra writeRaster
#' @export
fct_build_and_save_basemap <- function(data,
                                       estuary_name,
                                       villes_selection = NULL,
                                       path = "inst/extdata/basemaps") {

  # -------------------------
  # 0. Charger halin_table du package
  # -------------------------
  if (!exists("GPS_limits_haline")) {
    stop("GPS_limits_haline not found in the package")
  }

  halin_values <- GPS_limits_haline[GPS_limits_haline$estuary == estuary_name, ]
  halin_limit_lat <- halin_values$halin_limit_lat
  halin_limit_lon <- halin_values$halin_limit_lon
  
  # -------------------------
  # 1. Convert to sf
  # -------------------------
  points_sf <- data |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(latitude) & !is.na(longitude)) |>
    sf::st_as_sf(
      coords = c("longitude", "latitude"),
      crs = 4326,
      remove = FALSE
    )

  # -------------------------
  # 2. Bounding box + buffer
  # -------------------------
  bbox <- sf::st_bbox(points_sf)
  buffer_deg <- 0.02

  xlim <- c(bbox["xmin"] - buffer_deg,
            bbox["xmax"] + buffer_deg)

  ylim <- c(bbox["ymin"] - buffer_deg,
            bbox["ymax"] + buffer_deg)
  
  fixed_bbox <- fix_bbox_aspect(
  xlim = xlim,
  ylim = ylim,
  target_ratio = 1 # carte carrée
)

xlim <- fixed_bbox$xlim
ylim <- fixed_bbox$ylim

  coords <- matrix(
    c(
      xlim[1], ylim[1],
      xlim[1], ylim[2],
      xlim[2], ylim[2],
      xlim[2], ylim[1],
      xlim[1], ylim[1]
    ),
    ncol = 2,
    byrow = TRUE
  )

  poly <- sf::st_polygon(list(coords))
  bbox_sf <- sf::st_sf(
    geometry = sf::st_sfc(poly, crs = 4326)
  )

  bbox_vector <- c(
    xmin = xlim[1],
    ymin = ylim[1],
    xmax = xlim[2],
    ymax = ylim[2]
  )

  # -------------------------
  # 3. OSM cities
  # -------------------------
  villes_osm <- osmdata::opq(bbox = bbox_vector) |>
    osmdata::add_osm_feature(
      key = "place",
      value = c("city", "town", "village")
    ) |>
    osmdata::osmdata_sf()

  villes_points <- villes_osm$osm_points

  if (!is.null(villes_points) && nrow(villes_points) > 0) {

    villes_points <- sf::st_intersection(
      villes_points,
      sf::st_as_sfc(sf::st_bbox(points_sf))
    )

    # -------------------------
    #  Filtrage par noms fournis
    # -------------------------
    if (!is.null(villes_selection)) {

      villes_points <- villes_points |>
        dplyr::filter(name %in% villes_selection)

      # message si certaines villes absentes
      villes_manquantes <- setdiff(villes_selection,
                                   unique(villes_points$name))

      if (length(villes_manquantes) > 0) {
        warning("These cities were not found in OSM data: ",
                paste(villes_manquantes, collapse = ", "))
      }
    }

    villes_finales <- villes_points

  } else {
    villes_finales <- NULL
  }

  # -------------------------
  # 4. Download OSM raster
  # -------------------------
  osm_raster <- maptiles::get_tiles(
    bbox_sf,
    provider = "OpenStreetMap",
    crop = TRUE
  )

  # matérialisation pour éviter external pointer
  osm_raster <- terra::writeRaster(
    osm_raster,
    filename = tempfile(fileext = ".tif"),
    overwrite = TRUE
  )

  # -------------------------
  # 5. Save to package
  # -------------------------
  dir_estuary <- file.path(path, estuary_name)
  dir.create(dir_estuary,
             recursive = TRUE,
             showWarnings = FALSE)

  terra::writeRaster(
    osm_raster,
    filename = file.path(dir_estuary, "osm.tif"),
    overwrite = TRUE
  )

  if (!is.null(villes_finales)) {
    sf::st_write(
      villes_finales,
      file.path(dir_estuary, "villes.gpkg"),
      delete_dsn = TRUE,
      quiet = TRUE
    )
  }

  saveRDS(
    list(xlim = xlim, ylim = ylim,
         halin_limit_lat = halin_limit_lat,
         halin_limit_lon = halin_limit_lon),
    file.path(dir_estuary, "bbox.rds")
  )

  # -------------------------
  # 6. Return object
  # -------------------------
  return(list(
    points_sf = points_sf,
    osm_raster = osm_raster,
    villes = villes_finales,
    xlim = xlim,
    ylim = ylim,
    halin_limit_lat = halin_limit_lat,
    halin_limit_lon = halin_limit_lon
  ))
}
```


# load_estuary_basemap

```{r function-load_estuary_basemap}
#' Load estuary basemap (OpenStreetMap)
#' 
#' @param estuary_name Character name of estuary
#' @param package Name of package (default = "esteem.overview")
#'
#' @return List containing osm_raster, villes, xlim, ylim
#' 
#' @export
load_estuary_basemap <- function(estuary_name,
                                 package = "esteem.overview") {

  base_path <- system.file("extdata/basemaps",
                           estuary_name,
                           package = package)

  if (base_path == "") {
    stop("Estuary not found in package extdata/")
  }

  # Raster
  osm_raster_path <- file.path(base_path, "osm.tif")

  if (!file.exists(osm_raster_path)) {
    stop("osm.tif not found for this estuary.")
  }

  osm_raster <- terra::rast(osm_raster_path)

  # Cities
  villes_path <- file.path(base_path, "villes.gpkg")

  if (file.exists(villes_path)) {
    villes <- sf::st_read(villes_path, quiet = TRUE)
  } else {
    villes <- NULL
  }

  # Bbox
  bbox_path <- file.path(base_path, "bbox.rds")

  if (!file.exists(bbox_path)) {
    stop("bbox.rds not found for this estuary.")
  }

  bbox <- readRDS(bbox_path)

  return(list(
    osm_raster = osm_raster,
    villes = villes,
    xlim = bbox$xlim,
    ylim = bbox$ylim,
    halin_limit_lat = bbox$halin_limit_lat,
    halin_limit_lon = bbox$halin_limit_lon
  ))
}
```
  
# plot_estuary_map

```{r function-plot_estuary_map}
#' Plot estuary map with points, optional variable mapping and optional fixed aesthetics
#'
#' @param data Data frame containing latitude and longitude.
#' @param estuary_name Character name of the estuary.
#' @param colour_var Optional variable in `data`, or fixed colour value, for point outline colour.
#' @param colour_points Fixed point outline colour used when `colour_var` is not supplied.
#' @param fill_var Optional variable in `data`, or fixed colour value, for point fill.
#' @param fill_points Fixed point fill colour used when `fill_var` is not supplied.
#' @param size_var Optional variable in `data`, or fixed numeric value, for point size.
#' @param size_points Fixed point size used when `size_var` is not supplied.
#' @param shape_var Optional variable in `data`, or fixed shape value, for point shape.
#' @param shape_points Fixed point shape used when `shape_var` is not supplied.
#' @param package Package name where basemap is stored.
#'
#' @return ggplot object.
#' @export
plot_estuary_map <- function(data,
                             estuary_name,
                             colour_var,
                             colour_points = "black",
                             fill_var,
                             fill_points = NA,
                             size_var,
                             size_points = 0.6,
                             shape_var,
                             shape_points = 19,
                             package = "esteem.overview") {

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Helper: check whether an input is a valid colour value
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  is_colour_value <- function(x) {
    is.character(x) &&
      length(x) == 1 &&
      (
        grepl("^#(?:[0-9A-Fa-f]{3}){1,2}$", x) ||
          x %in% grDevices::colors()
      )
  }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Helper: resolve variable mapping or fixed aesthetic value
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  resolve_aesthetic <- function(var,
                                data,
                                type = c("colour", "fill", "size", "shape")) {
    type <- match.arg(type)

    quo <- rlang::enquo(var)
    expr <- rlang::get_expr(quo)

    # If the argument is an unquoted column name present in data,
    # treat it as a mapped aesthetic.
    if (rlang::is_symbol(expr)) {
      var_name <- rlang::as_string(expr)

      if (var_name %in% names(data)) {
        return(list(mapping = quo, value = NULL))
      }
    }

    # Otherwise, try to evaluate the argument as a fixed value.
    val <- tryCatch(
      rlang::eval_tidy(quo),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )

    is_fixed_value <- switch(
      type,
      colour = is_colour_value(val),
      fill   = is_colour_value(val) || (length(val) == 1 && is.na(val)),
      size   = is.numeric(val) && length(val) == 1,
      shape  = (is.numeric(val) || is.character(val)) && length(val) == 1
    )

    if (is_fixed_value) {
      list(mapping = NULL, value = val)
    } else {
      list(mapping = quo, value = NULL)
    }
  }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Load estuary basemap
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  basemap <- load_estuary_basemap(
    estuary_name,
    package = package
  )

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Convert sampling data to sf points
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  points_sf <- make_points_sf(data)

  # Keep points in WGS84 for consistency with the OSM raster.
  points_sf <- sf::st_transform(points_sf, 4326)

  points_coords <- cbind(
    sf::st_drop_geometry(points_sf),
    sf::st_coordinates(points_sf)
  )

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Prepare city locations
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  if (!is.null(basemap$villes) && nrow(basemap$villes) > 0) {
    basemap$villes <- sf::st_transform(
      basemap$villes,
      4326
    )
  }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Convert raster basemap to dataframe for ggplot
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  raster_df <- terra::as.data.frame(
    basemap$osm_raster,
    xy = TRUE,
    na.rm = FALSE
  )

  rgb_cols <- setdiff(
    names(raster_df),
    c("x", "y")
  )[1:3]

  raster_df$rgb <- grDevices::rgb(
    raster_df[[rgb_cols[1]]],
    raster_df[[rgb_cols[2]]],
    raster_df[[rgb_cols[3]]],
    maxColorValue = 255
  )

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Initialise base map
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  p <- ggplot2::ggplot() +
    ggplot2::geom_raster(
      data = raster_df,
      ggplot2::aes(
        x = x,
        y = y,
        fill = rgb
      )
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_fill_identity()

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Initialise point aesthetic defaults
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  mapping <- list()

  point_colour <- colour_points
  point_fill   <- fill_points
  point_size   <- size_points
  point_shape  <- shape_points

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Resolve point colour aesthetic
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  if (!missing(colour_var)) {
    colour_res <- resolve_aesthetic(
      {{ colour_var }},
      points_coords,
      type = "colour"
    )

    if (!is.null(colour_res$mapping)) {
      mapping$colour <- colour_res$mapping
      point_colour <- NULL
    } else {
      point_colour <- colour_res$value
    }
  }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Resolve point fill aesthetic
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  if (!missing(fill_var)) {
    fill_res <- resolve_aesthetic(
      {{ fill_var }},
      points_coords,
      type = "fill"
    )

    if (!is.null(fill_res$mapping)) {
      mapping$fill <- fill_res$mapping
      point_fill <- NULL
    } else {
      point_fill <- fill_res$value
    }
  }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Resolve point size aesthetic
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  if (!missing(size_var)) {
    size_res <- resolve_aesthetic(
      {{ size_var }},
      points_coords,
      type = "size"
    )

    if (!is.null(size_res$mapping)) {
      mapping$size <- size_res$mapping
      point_size <- NULL
    } else {
      point_size <- size_res$value
    }
  }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Resolve point shape aesthetic
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  if (!missing(shape_var)) {
    shape_res <- resolve_aesthetic(
      {{ shape_var }},
      points_coords,
      type = "shape"
    )

    if (!is.null(shape_res$mapping)) {
      mapping$shape <- shape_res$mapping
      point_shape <- NULL
    } else {
      point_shape <- shape_res$value
    }
  }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Select a fill-compatible default shape when fill is mapped
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  if (!"shape" %in% names(mapping) && "fill" %in% names(mapping)) {
    point_shape <- 21
  }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Build point aesthetic mapping
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  point_mapping <- c(
    list(
      x = quote(X),
      y = quote(Y)
    ),
    mapping
  )

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Build geom_point() arguments
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  point_args <- list(
    data = points_coords,
    mapping = do.call(ggplot2::aes, point_mapping),
    alpha = 0.8
  )

  if (!is.null(point_colour)) {
    point_args$colour <- point_colour
  }

  if (!is.null(point_fill)) {
    point_args$fill <- point_fill
  }

  if (!is.null(point_size)) {
    point_args$size <- point_size
  }

  if (!is.null(point_shape)) {
    point_args$shape <- point_shape
  }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Add sampling points
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  p <- p + do.call(ggplot2::geom_point, point_args)

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Add cities and city labels
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  if (!is.null(basemap$villes) && nrow(basemap$villes) > 0) {
    villes_coords <- cbind(
      sf::st_drop_geometry(basemap$villes),
      sf::st_coordinates(basemap$villes)
    )

    p <- p +
      ggplot2::geom_point(
        data = villes_coords,
        ggplot2::aes(
          x = X,
          y = Y
        ),
        shape = 21,
        fill = "black",
        colour = "black",
        size = 1.5
      ) +
      ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
        data = villes_coords,
        ggplot2::aes(
          x = X,
          y = Y,
          label = name
        ),
        min.segment.length = 0,
        nudge_y = 0.01,
        size = 2.3,
        colour = "black",
        family = "sans"
      )
  }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Prepare salinity limit coordinates
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  line_frac <- 0.45

  x_mid <- mean(basemap$xlim)
  y_mid <- mean(basemap$ylim)

  x_half <- diff(basemap$xlim) * line_frac / 2
  y_half <- diff(basemap$ylim) * line_frac / 2

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Add horizontal salinity limit when available
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  if (!is.null(basemap$halin_limit_lat) &&
      !is.na(basemap$halin_limit_lat)) {
    line_data <- data.frame(
      x = x_mid - x_half,
      xend = x_mid + x_half,
      y = basemap$halin_limit_lat,
      yend = basemap$halin_limit_lat
    )

    p <- p +
      ggplot2::geom_segment(
        data = line_data,
        ggplot2::aes(
          x = x,
          xend = xend,
          y = y,
          yend = yend
        ),
        colour = "black",
        linewidth = 0.8
      )
  }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Add vertical salinity limit when available
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  if (!is.null(basemap$halin_limit_lon) &&
      !is.na(basemap$halin_limit_lon)) {
    line_data <- data.frame(
      x = basemap$halin_limit_lon,
      xend = basemap$halin_limit_lon,
      y = y_mid - y_half,
      yend = y_mid + y_half
    )

    p <- p +
      ggplot2::geom_segment(
        data = line_data,
        ggplot2::aes(
          x = x,
          xend = xend,
          y = y,
          yend = yend
        ),
        colour = "black",
        linewidth = 0.8
      )
  }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Set map extent
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  raster_xlim <- range(raster_df$x, na.rm = TRUE)
  raster_ylim <- range(raster_df$y, na.rm = TRUE)

  p <- p +
    ggplot2::coord_cartesian(
      xlim = raster_xlim,
      ylim = raster_ylim,
      expand = FALSE
    )

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Apply theme and axis formatting
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  p <- p +
    theme_esteem() +
    ggplot2::theme(
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 9),
      axis.title = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      axis.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 6)
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(
      labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(
      labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
    )

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Add plot title
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  p <- p +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = paste0(estuary_name, " estuary")
    )

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Return final plot
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  return(p)
}

```

# get_mean_gps_position
    
```{r function-get_mean_gps_position}
#' Get mean GPS positions from different points
#' 
#' @return tibble summarised data with mean_latitude and mean_longitude variables
#' 
#' @noRd
get_mean_gps_position <- function(data, latitude, longitude){
  
  data_mean_positions <- data |>
    summarise(
      longitude = mean(longitude, na.rm = TRUE),
      latitude = mean(latitude, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  
  return(data_mean_positions)
}
```

# filter_studied_gps_boxes

```{r function-filter_studied_gps_boxes}
#' Filter data subset corresponding of GPS boxes to be studied
#' 
#' @param data tibble with estuary, haline_zone, latitude and longitude variables
#'
#' @return tibble filtered for GPS boxes to be thurther studied
#' 
#' @noRd
filter_studied_gps_boxes <- function(data) {
  filtered_data <- data |>
    filter(
      case_when(
        estuary == "Gironde" & haline_zone == "outer" &
          latitude >= 45.48 &
          latitude < 45.56 & longitude > -1.1 & longitude < -0.9 ~ TRUE,
        estuary == "Gironde" & haline_zone == "inner" &
          latitude >= 45.15 &
          latitude < 45.30 & longitude > -0.8 & longitude < -0.6 ~ TRUE,
        estuary == "Loire" & haline_zone == "outer" &
          latitude >= 47.26 &
          latitude < 47.30 & longitude > -2.2 & longitude < -2.15 ~ TRUE,
        estuary == "Loire" & haline_zone == "inner" &
          latitude >= 47.27 &
          latitude < 47.29 & longitude > -2.0 & longitude < -1.8 ~ TRUE,
        estuary == "Seine" & haline_zone == "outer" &
          latitude >= 49.42 &
          latitude < 49.47 & longitude > 0.07 & longitude < 0.2 ~ TRUE,
        estuary == "Seine" & haline_zone == "inner" &
          latitude >= 49.42 &
          latitude < 49.49 & longitude > 0.2 ~ TRUE,
        TRUE ~ FALSE
      )
    )
  
  return(filtered_data)
}
```
 
# plot_maps_parameter_years

```{r function-plot_maps_parameter_years}
#' Map yearly mean GPS position by estuary and haline zone
#' 
#' @param data tibble, data containing PARAMETRE_LIBELLE, estuary, year, haline_zone, latitude, longitude
#' @param parameter character, name of the parameter GPS measurment positions to be mapped
#' @param filename character, complete path where to save the map
#'
#' @return saved ggplot
#' 
#' @export
plot_maps_parameter_years <- function(data, parameter, filename){
  
  map_mean_positions <- data |>
    filter(PARAMETRE_LIBELLE == parameter) |>
    drop_na(RESULTAT) |> 
    group_by(estuary, year, haline_zone) |>
    get_mean_gps_position()

p1 <- plot_estuary_map(data = map_mean_positions,
                 estuary_name = "Gironde", 
                 colour_var = year, size_points = 2) +
  scale_colour_viridis_c(option = "plasma", end = 0.95)

p2 <- plot_estuary_map(data = map_mean_positions,
                 estuary_name = "Loire", 
                 colour_var = year, size_points = 2) +
  scale_colour_viridis_c(option = "plasma", end = 0.95)

p3 <- plot_estuary_map(data = map_mean_positions,
                 estuary_name = "Seine", 
                 colour_var = year,  size_points = 2) +
  scale_colour_viridis_c(option = "plasma", end = 0.95)

ggplot_parameter_map <- plot_grid(p1, p2, p3, ncol = 1)


ggsave(plot = ggplot_parameter_map,
       filename = filename,
       height = 20, width = 15, units = "cm")
}
```
  
  
# Inflate

```{r development-inflate, eval=FALSE}
# Run but keep eval=FALSE to avoid infinite loop
# Execute in the console directly
fusen::inflate(flat_file = "dev/flat_maps.Rmd", 
               vignette_name = NULL, check = FALSE)
```
